* Fix MLP underfitting: gate continuity_constant to decay phase only

The continuity_constant (C = g(tau)/g(tau+1)) was applied during ALL
training phases, including the constant phase (t <= tau). This inflated
the effective learning rate unnecessarily during the constant phase.

Fix: apply C only during the decay phase (t > tau); use C=1.0 during
the constant phase. This eliminates the spurious LR inflation and
improves training stability for hybrid rate functions.

commit.message:
fix(core): add resource limits and harden production WASM safety (#26)

* fix(core): add resource limits and harden production WASM safety

- Add src/limits.rs with MAX_CONFIG_BYTES (100MB), MAX_CONTEXT_BYTES (1MB),
  MAX_REF_DEPTH (64), MAX_JSON_DEPTH (128) and check_json_depth() validator
- Enforce config and context size limits at the WASM FFI boundary in
  update_state_internal and evaluate_internal before any memory reads
- Add JSON nesting depth pre-parse scan in FlagEvaluator::update_state to
  prevent stack overflows; applied to both JSON and YAML (post-conversion) paths
- Add $ref hop-depth counter to resolve_refs (depth counts only $ref hops,
  not structural JSON traversal)
- Fix mutex.lock().unwrap() -> unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) in the
  native non-WASM evaluator module to survive mutex poisoning gracefully
- Eliminate double get_state() call and production .unwrap() in
  evaluate_internal by binding state once
- Add adversarial tests: oversized config/context, deeply nested JSON,
  deep $ref chains, and circular $ref regression
- Document all limits and enforcement points in ARCHITECTURE.md

Closes #23

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(core): extract parse_context_from_memory helper to remove duplication

Resolve the review comment on PR #26: the context-parsing logic
(size check → UTF-8 read → JSON parse) was duplicated verbatim in both
evaluate_by_index_internal and evaluate_internal.

Extract it into a single private helper

Both callers now delegate to this helper.  evaluate_internal retains its
extra short-circuit (flag has no targeting rules → skip parsing) as a
plain if/else around the helper call.

Fix MLP underfitting: normalize LR denominator by sample size (#30)

* Initial plan

* Fix MLP underfitting: normalize LR denominator by sample size (1/N averaging)

The adaptive learning rate denominator used a raw sum over N training
samples but the loss gradient uses a 1/N mean (MSE). This mismatch
made the effective step size inversely proportional to N, causing
near-zero updates and severe underfitting.

Fix: divide the denominator by sample_size so the effective step size
is independent of N. With the Forrester benchmark (depth=3, width=100,
N=100, 1000 epochs), the decay-LR model's final MSE drops from ~19
(near-zero predictions) to ~0.70 (good fit).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/kyleosung/StepByStep-AdaptiveGDForLipschitzNNs_DEV/sessions/62c76fc9-2279-482a-8960-b0b27db49051

fix: prevent mssql shared.driver pollution between Windows and SQL auth pools

require('mssql/msnodesqlv8') overwrites the global shared.driver singleton
(lib/shared.js) used by ALL pools' pool.request() / pool.transaction() calls.
After a failed Windows-auth attempt, any SQL-auth (tedious) pool created in
the same process would produce msnodesqlv8 Request objects on tedious
connections, causing 'connection.queryRaw is not a function'.

Fix:
- Capture tedious Request/Transaction/ConnectionPool at module load time
  (before msnodesqlv8 can overwrite shared.driver)
- After creating each msnodesqlv8 pool, call _restoreSharedDriverToTedious()
  to reset shared.driver back to tedious
- Patch the msnodesqlv8 pool's request() and transaction() to use the
  msnodesqlv8 classes directly, since shared.driver no longer points to them

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
remove ConventionPlugin (default built-in)

The ConventionDiscovery Step 2 matched FK columns by exact name equality
with the PK column (colName === pkColumn). Investigation of the full CW
schema (1,360 tables, 2,798 FK constraints) shows this never matches in
practice — FK columns are always named differently from the PK they
reference (e.g. COL_COO → COO_PK). The plugin was broken against real
data and caught zero relationships.

The 13 soft FKs it was meant to catch (<0.5% of all relationships) are
not worth the dead-code overhead. ConstraintDiscovery covers all 2,798
declared FKs; the polymorphic plugin covers ParentTableCode/ParentID.

Plugin architecture remains fully modular — PluginRegistry, pluginTypes,
and the external-plugin loading path are all untouched. A future plugin
(built-in or external) can implement convention-based discovery correctly
without touching the core.

Removed:
- src/main/plugins/definitions/ConventionPlugin.ts
- src/main/plugins/discovery/ConventionDiscovery.ts

Edited:
- src/main/index.ts: remove import and registration
- src/shared/definitions/constants.ts: remove convention from DEFAULT_PLUGIN_SETTINGS
- src/main/plugins/traversal/SimpleJoinTraversal.ts: update comment
- src/shared/definitions/pluginTypes.ts: update comment

Replaced absolute-positioning + ResizeObserver approach with a flex top bar.

commit.message:
audit 5.4: fix FavouritesPanel reactivity via FavouriteItem sub-component

FavouriteItem checkboxes were reading checked state via
getState().isFavouriteActive() — an imperative snapshot with no Zustand
subscription. Checkboxes only updated correctly by accident, because
ancestor components (AppShell via useVisibilityDerived) happened to
subscribe to overlapping store values and cascade a re-render down.
This would silently break if the ancestor subscription changed or any
parent was memoised.

Fix: extract a private FavouriteItem sub-component that subscribes to
its own active state via a proper useSettingsStore selector. Each item
re-renders independently when its underlying setting changes.
executeFavourite and removeFavourite remain as getState() calls —
correct, since they are fire-and-forget mutations, not reads.

- FavouritesPanel.tsx: extract FavouriteItem with useSettingsStore
  selector for isActive; import Favourite type explicitly
- tests: add verify-audit-5.4-item3.spec.ts covering star, state
  reflection, bidirectional reactivity, and cleanup

[paper] Rewrite Paper 8: replace power-law narrative with two-channel regular expansion

Replace the failed power-law asymptotic (kappa ~ C*eps^-alpha) with the
correct two-channel regular expansion framework per Dietrich/Volkov/Lindqvist:

  sigma_min(eps) = sigma_0 + lambda_1*eps^2 + lambda_2*eps^4 + O(eps^6)

where sigma_0 > 0 is the curvature channel (mode-dependent Legendre weights
break a/c degeneracy even at the sphere) and lambda_1*eps^2 is the shape
channel (eccentricity-driven).

Key changes:
- Abstract: rewrite with two-channel mechanism, three-class classification,
  oblate-prolate asymmetry, explicit (five modes) labels
- Theory: replace power-law equation with regular expansion (eq 19-20),
  cite Kato 1966 and Stewart & Sun 1990
- Results: replace power-law fit section with expansion analysis reporting
  sigma_0=0.01113, lambda_1=0.00507, eps_c=1.48>1; reframe fitted alpha
  as effective local exponent not asymptotic law
- Discussion: replace Conjecture 1 (power-law rate) with three-class
  classification (singular/regular bounded/non-lifting); add new
  oblate-prolate asymmetry subsection explaining curvature-mode
  anti-correlation mechanism
- Conclusion: replace power-law target with classification as main
  theoretical contribution
- References: add Stewart & Sun 1990; change companion papers to
  @unpublished; fix Leissa1973 NASA caps; remove orphaned Sarvazyan1998;
  fix Aernouts2012 misuse (tympanic membrane cited for abdominal wall)
- Introduction: update power-law bullet to two-channel expansion
- README: update Paper 8 draft link

All 482 tests pass. Paper compiles cleanly (24pp).

* feat: implement all 6 feature improvements from issue

1. Per-Parameter Scaling Constants: Add per_param_constants parameter to GDTrainer
   with C=1 default, update docstring documenting the continuity guarantee
2. Fix Hybrid Logarithmic Rate Function: Update formula to use
   g(t) = λ / (1 + log(1 + t - τ))^r matching the paper's template
3. LR Continuity: Documented in GDTrainer docstring that C=1 + hybrid
   rate function continuity at τ preserves LR continuity
4. Batched Lipschitz Estimation: Add batch_size parameter to
   estimate_lipschitz_constant() with automatic OOM recovery

commit.message:
Update createMeter API to pass fixed/system dimension values (#562)

- Change `IFabricMeterProvider.CreateMeter` method to pass fixed
dimension values on creation, to improve performance issues. Note that
this is safe because `systemDimensions` are fixed and are stored when
`MeterProvider` is created. Interface change itself is safe since the
feature is still in development and we don't have to worry about the
backwards comparibility.
- Pass only customDimension values to the interop in `Meter.Record`,
since fixed (system) dimensions values are already known on creation.
- Delete `systemDimensionValues` field from `Meter `since it is now
unused

Native optimization changes that require this PR to integrate:
[PR](https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_git/WindowsFabric/pullrequest/15120943?path=/src/prod/src/Telemetry/Native/FabricMeter.cpp)
